children.name: Capito
children.description: Medium-sized, heavily-built barbets with thick, bristled bills and strikingly patterned plumage in red, yellow, white, black, and various shades of brown. They are primarily frugivorous, playing an important role in seed dispersal in Neotropical forests. Males and females often differ markedly in plumage pattern.

children.name: Capito wallacei
children.description: A spectacular barbet discovered only in 1996 in the cloud forests of the Cordillera Azul in Peru. It has a black head and upperparts, a brilliant white throat and breast, and a vivid scarlet band across the upper breast. Its restricted range and specialised habitat make it highly vulnerable to deforestation.

children.name: Capito auratus
children.description: Found in the western Amazon basin from Colombia and Venezuela to northern Bolivia. The male has a black cap, a yellow-orange face and underparts, and a heavily striped black-and-white back. It inhabits humid lowland forests and is one of the most frequently encountered barbets in Amazonia.

children.name: Capito aurovirens
children.description: A distinctive barbet of the western Amazon, found in eastern Ecuador, Peru, and western Brazil. The male has a bright red crown, a white face, and a black and white body with yellow underparts. It favours vÃ¡rzea and gallery forests along major rivers.

children.name: Capito brunneipectus
children.description: A barbet with brown chest from the southern Amazon.

children.name: Capito dayi
children.description: A barbet with black girdle from the Amazon basin.

children.name: Capito hypoleucus
children.description: A barbet with white mantle from Colombia.

children.name: Capito maculicoronatus
children.description: A barbet with spotted crown from Panama and Colombia.

children.name: Capito niger
children.description: A barbet with black spots from the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Capito quinticolor
children.description: A multi-coloured barbet from the Choco region.

children.name: Capito striatus
children.description: A barbet with fine streaking from the western Amazon.

children.name: Capito quinticolor
children.description: A multi-coloured barbet from the Choco region.

children.name: Eubucco
children.description: Small, colourful barbets with striking sexual dimorphism, found in the humid forests of Central and South America. They are among the most vividly coloured of all barbets, with males typically sporting red, yellow, green and blue in bold patterns.

children.name: Eubucco richardsoni
children.description: A small barbet of the western Amazon basin, found in Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Brazil. The male has a striking pattern of red head, yellow throat, green upperparts, and yellow belly with red and orange streaks. It favours the canopy of lowland rainforest and is more often heard than seen.

children.name: Eubucco boucerii
children.description: A barbet from the Choco region.

children.name: Eubucco tucinkae
children.description: A barbet with scarlet hood from the western Amazon.

children.name: Eubucco versicolor
children.description: A multi-coloured barbet from the Andes of Peru and Bolivia.
